entries.cleaned_md:
This instance uses the same map as the menagerie.

## Trash & Layout

1. **Main Gallery**
   - There is a wandering mob of a Skeletal Patrol Leader and 4 Skeletal Warrior (lvl 50^^^ epic(x2)). The pathing will take this mob up to each doorway. They are on a repop timer of about 8 min.
   - The doors to the two side chambers and center chamber are guarded by 2 Fettered Guardian and 2 enslaved Thrall (lvl 48^^^ epic(x2)). These guards are also on a repop timer of about 10 min. Be careful with AOE close to the side chamber doors because you can pull another set of guards from inside the side chambers.
2. **Right hand chamber**
   - Just inside the doorway is guarded by 2 Fettered Guardian and 2 enslaved Thrall (lvl 48^^^ epic(x2)). On the bed is a Ar'ticae Cultist guarded by 2 Fettered Guardian and 2 enslaved Thrall (lvl 48^^^ epic(x2)). Clear this mob to open the other chamber door.
3. **Left hand chamber**
   - Just inside the doorway is guarded by 2 Fettered Guardian and 2 enslaved Thrall (lvl 50^^^ epic(x2)). Behind the crystal is a Ar'ticae Scryer guarded by 2 Fettered Guardian and 2 enslaved Thrall (lvl 50^^^ epic(x2)). Clear this mob to open the center vault chamber door.
4. **Center Vault**
   - The door to the Vault is guarded by Raegh the Protector (lvl 52^^^ epic(x4)).
   - Inside the vault the walls are flanked by 6 Ice Protector (lvl 51 ^^^).
   - Behind Ar'ticae (who is non-aggro) are an Ar'ticae Summoner and 2 Fettered Guardian and 2 enslaved Thrall (lvl 51^^^ epic(x2)). There is also a lamina Vaner (lvl 51). Once you clear this mob, Ar'ticae will immediately become aggro.

entries.zone_name: The Bloodskull Valley: A Noble Confrontation
entries.cleaned_md:
## Zone Gimmick

This zone throws a curve-ball compared to the others. After killing the first couple trash at the first wall you come up to, there is a zonewide announcement that the Spotter calls for reinforcements. This will send a bunch of orc adds, and will not let up until you get into the tower and kill the Spotter.

You have to take the adds as you go, pull one Epic encounter at a time, all the while killing the adds and rarely being out of combat. Mana regen and conservation here is a MUST, as well as moving on quickly once the Epic encounter is dead (whether the adds are necessarily all gone or not). The Orc Mages also power drain. They make good charmed pets.

## Tower Trash

Do not AoE at the top as there are Archers there as well. They will hit you through walls, extremely far away as well, and can't be avoided short of killing them off. They have a ton of hitpoints compared to the regular Epic encounters. They also don't aggro properly (they don't move around and no swinging action) and will continue to do the arrow attack at random people while targeting the MT, so it can be hard to see if they've aggro'd and who they are attacking.

Fights in the tower can be a little buggy as the mobs will sometimes be on a different floor and not visible, but can still attack/be attacked.

## Progression

Once the first Spotter is dead, the rest is easier. Head back towards the entrance, and go south along the zone wall (not the short wall) towards the second tower. If you approach while keeping to the edge of the zone, the Spotter won't see you and send adds until you are practically on top of him. Kill him quick and take down those archers as well if you want to stop them. The orc leaders that wander up and down the wall can be ignored as they don't drop anything or set off anything, they just yell orders constantly.

> Do not try and take Chief Surveyor Remolus first, as he has some massive AE that vaporizes the raid if the Commander isn't killed first.

entries.zone_name: The Cove of Decay: A Heroic Confrontation
entries.cleaned_md:
## Zone Gimmick

Zone will split an x2 raid into two separate, parallel zones. Balance your groups before you go in, and be prepared to 1-group the zone.

## Trash

The zone will be rampant with undead, however they can mostly be ignored by running along the wall.

## Access

The secret to defeating the DM is the djinn weakness to music. This is hinted at throughout the lore, so before entering you will have wanted to complete the quest [The Handcrafted Music Box](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Handcrafted_Music_Box_(Quest)). Having the reward from this quest will trigger the next part.

## Progression

Upon entering zone a message will appear telling a hint of what song is needed to defeat the DM. Named on the way to the master will drop the song needed.

## Trash

Carefully pull the first two guards into the stairway zone in, they're very social and can add two more guards easily; all four can be killed at the same time with a mid 80's and above raid.

Once inside clear the trash mobs around the room by pulling them one at a time up to the raid near the entry killing the champion last down on the main floor. (Which actually leaves Pawbuster for last.)

entries.zone_name: The Firemyst Gully: A Gallant Confrontation
entries.cleaned_md:
## Zone Gimmick

Zone will split an x2 raid into two separate, parallel zones. Balance your groups before you go in, and be prepared to 1-group the zone.

This is a tier 2 Kunark raiding zone.

## Trash

Once inside the Temple you will need to watch out for wandering 83x4 mobs. There are 5 or 6 of these wandering mobs which come in pairs roaming throughout the zone, and they roam a LONG way. One pair of these mobs will come almost all the way to the zone in point. They come in pairs (a Charnak soldier and a Chokidai tracker) but may be far apart -- have the **MT** taunt the soldier and the **OT** taunt the tracker.

## Progression

From the zone in point your raid force will need to head either left or right up the stairs. No wandering mobs up there, just make sure your raid force avoids pulling the first wandering mob as you proceed up the stairs. Kill the 83x4 a runic guardian (non-aggro simple tank and spank) in both the room to the right and the room to the left. Go back to the entrance and proceed to kill the first wandering pair then proceed into the first lower room to kill another Runic Guardian.

Proceed into the next hallway, continuing to keep an eye out for wandering pairs. The next room is a non-attackable Atrebe. Kill off a couple of the other wandering pairs of Soldier/dog combos. Enter into the next hallway making your first available left down yet another hallway. Follow this hallway around to a circular room and kill the Runic Guardian that stands there. Proceed down the stairs all the way and kill more Runic Guardians in the room to the left and right.

## Spawning Atrebe

After the fight in the room with the excavationers, Uthtak the Cruel, and Aktar the Dark, in the room they were standing is a small box that your **Monk**, **Bruiser** or **SK** should collect for the next fight.

Everyone now moves back to the unattackable Atrebe's room. The person who collected the box can spawn Atrebe.

## Atrium & Twins

Continue the way you were going until you reach the atrium -- it will have 4 waterfalls in the corners. Have the raid stay in the hallway while the **MT** pulls the roamer back to the raid for an easy kill. There are two assassins hidden in the waterfalls. Have the MT step into the water to spawn them and pull them back to the raid for some more easy kills.

There are two smaller hallways to the north and east. Each end in a room that contains a named, Zarda and Kodux. Pull the extra mob in each room and kill them in the hallway before beginning the next fight.

## Final Approach

Head down the west hallway, killing another set of roamers and two more Runic Guardians.

## Composition

It might be smart to have two solid tanks for this raid. It's advisable that you have a **Crusader** with the [Fearless Morale](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Fearless_Morale) AA ability. Success in this zone depends greatly on the raid's ability to withstand three things: **fear**, **stun**, and the **cocoons (crushing)**.

## Trash

Clearing the entire zone of trash mobs (drachnids and skeletons) is the first goal. All skeletons will fear, so crouching and walking while fighting the skeletons will minimize the impact of this when it happens. Try to make these every 2nd pull, so the MT templar can have [Sanctuary](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sanctuary) up.
